src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDb module

Bases: object

__init__(host=None, module_name=None, dbname='OSIR_db', port=5432)[source]

” Central PostgreSQL service for the OSIR framework.

close()[source]

Cleanly close the connection.

execute_query(query, params=None, fetch=None, max_retries=5)[source]

Executes a query with automatic reconnection and retry logic.

src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbCase module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbCase.OsirDbCase(db_osir)[source]

Bases: object

Manages the lifecycle of forensic cases within the PostgreSQL database.

The OsirDbCase handles the creation, retrieval, and deletion of case metadata.

__init__(db_osir)[source]

Initializes the OsirDbCase with an active database connection.

Parameters:

db_osir (OsirDb) – An instance of the OSIR database service.

create(name: str) → OsirDbCaseModel[source]

Registers a new case in the database or retrieves an existing one.

Parameters:

name (str) – The human-readable name of the forensic case.

Returns:

The OsirDbCaseModel associated with the case.

Return type:

OsirDbCaseModel

create_table()[source]

Initializes the ‘osir_case’ table in the database if it does not exist.

The table structure ensures that case names are unique to prevent data collision between different investigations.

delete(case_uuid: str) → bool[source]

Removes a case record from the database.

Parameters:

case_uuid (str) – The UUID of the case to remove.

Returns:

True if the operation was successful.

Return type:

bool

get(case_uuid: str = None, name: str = None) → OsirDbCaseModel | None[source]

Retrieves a case UUID based on either the ID or the name.

Parameters:

  • case_uuid (str, optional) – The UUID of the case.

  • name (str, optional) – The name of the case.

Returns:

The found UUID or None if no match is found.

Return type:

Union[str, None]

Raises:

ValueError – If neither case_uuid nor name is provided.

list() → List[OsirDbCaseModel][source]

Lists all forensic cases stored in the database.

Returns:

A list of OsirDbCaseModel(case_uuid, name).

Return type:

List[OsirDbCaseModel]

src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbConstants module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbConstants.ProcessingStatus(*values)[source]

Bases: str, Enum

PROCESSING_DONE = 'processing_done'
PROCESSING_FAILED = 'processing_failed'
PROCESSING_STARTED = 'processing_started'
TASK_CREATED = 'task_created'

src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbHandler module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbHandler.OsirDbHandler(db_osir)[source]

Bases: object

Manages high-level execution handlers within the OSIR database.

A ‘Handler’ represents a grouped execution of one or more forensic modules for a specific case.

__init__(db_osir)[source]

Initializes the OsirDbHandler with a database connection.

append_task_ids(handler_id: str, new_task_ids: List[str]) → bool[source]

Dynamically adds new Celery task IDs to an existing handler. Useful when a module discovery process triggers additional tasks.

check_handler_failure(handler_id: str) → bool[source]

Aggregates task health: returns True if any associated task has failed.

create(case_uuid: str, modules: List[str], task_ids: List[str], handler_id: UUID = None) → OsirDbHandlerModel[source]

Registers a new execution handler.

Parameters:

  • case_uuid (str) – The UUID of the investigation case.

  • modules (List[str]) – Names of the modules included in this execution.

  • task_ids (List[str]) – Initial Celery task IDs associated with this handler.

  • handler_id (uuid.UUID, optional) – A specific UUID to use, otherwise a new one is generated.

Returns:

The unique handler_id.

Return type:

str

create_table()[source]

Initializes the ‘osir_handlers’ table in PostgreSQL.

delete(handler_id: str | None = None, case_uuid: str | None = None, task_id: str | None = None) → bool[source]

Deletes handlers or removes specific task references.

get(handler_id: str | None = None, case_uuid: str | None = None) → OsirDbHandlerModel | List[OsirDbHandlerModel] | None[source]

Retrieves one or more handlers by ID or Case UUID.

is_processing_active(handler_uuid: str) → bool[source]

Checks if any tasks associated with a specific handler are still in a pending or active state.

Parameters:

handler_uuid (str) – The UUID of the handler to check.

Returns:

True if any associated tasks are ‘task_created’ or ‘processing_started’.

Return type:

bool

Raises:

Exception – If the join query between handlers and tasks fails.

list(case_uuid: str | None = None, processing_status: str | List[str] | None = None, exclude_status: str | List[str] | None = None) → List[OsirDbHandlerModel][source]

Retrieves a filtered list of handlers, joining with case metadata.

update(handler_id: str, processing_status: str) → bool[source]

Updates the global status of a handler.

src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbSnapshot module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbSnapshot.OsirDbSnapshot(db_osir)[source]

Bases: object

__init__(db_osir)[source]
create_table()[source]
get_stored_case_snapshot(case_path: str) → List[Tuple[str, str]][source]

Retrieves all file/directory entries stored for a specific case path from the snapshot.

Parameters:

case_path (str) – The path of the case to look up.

Returns:

A list of tuples containing (path, entry_type).

Return type:

List[Tuple[str, str]]

store_case_snapshot(case_uuid: str, case_path: str, entries_list: List[Tuple[str, str]])[source]

Performs a bulk update of the case snapshot. Clears existing records for the case and uses a fast COPY command to insert new unique entries.

Parameters:

  • case_uuid (str) – The unique identifier of the case.

  • case_path (str) – The root path of the case.

  • entries_list (List[Tuple[str, str]]) – A list of (path, entry_type) to be stored.

Note

This method filters out duplicate paths and paths containing backslashes before performing the bulk insert via a buffer.

Raises:

  • OperationalError – If the database connection cannot be established.

  • Exception – For any errors during the bulk copy process.

src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbTask module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.postgres.OsirDbTask.OsirDbTask(db_osir)[source]

Bases: object

Manages the lifecycle of tasks stored in the PostgreSQL ‘osir_tasks’ table, including table creation, task creation, retrieval, updates, and deletion.

__init__(db_osir)[source]
check_input(case_uuid: str, input: str) → bool[source]

Checks if there is currently an active task (status ‘processing_started’) with the same input for a specific case.

Parameters:

  • case_uuid (str) – The UUID of the case.

  • input (str) – The input string to check for duplicates.

Returns:

True if a matching active task is found, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

create(case_uuid: str, agent: str, module: str, input: str, output: str = 'N/A', task_id: str | None = None) → str[source]

Inserts a new task into the database.

Parameters:

  • case_uuid (str) – The UUID of the case associated with the task.

  • agent (str) – The name or identifier of the agent performing the task.

  • module (str) – The specific module responsible for the execution.

  • input (str) – The input data or configuration for the task.

  • output (str) – The output path of the module

  • task_id (str, optional) – A specific UUID for the task. If None, a new UUID is generated.

Returns:

The UUID of the created task.

Return type:

str

Raises:

Exception – If the database insertion fails.

create_table()[source]

Creates the ‘osir_tasks’ table and the custom ENUM type ‘processing_status_enum’ if they do not already exist.

Raises:

Exception – If the database query fails.

delete(task_id: str | None = None, case_uuid: str | None = None, handler_id: str | None = None) → bool[source]

Deletes tasks from the table based on either task_id, case_uuid, or handler_id.

Parameters:

  • task_id (str, optional) – The ID of the specific task to delete.

  • case_uuid (str, optional) – The UUID of the case to delete all related tasks for.

  • handler_id (str, optional) – The ID of the handler whose tasks should be deleted.

Returns:

True if the deletion was successful.

Return type:

bool

Raises:

  • ValueError – If none of the optional parameters are provided.

  • Exception – If the deletion query fails.

get(task_id: str) → OsirDbTaskModel[source]

Retrieves a single task’s details by its UUID.

Parameters:

task_id (str) – The UUID of the task to retrieve.

Returns:

A dictionary containing task details, or None if no task is found or task_id is empty.

Return type:

Union[dict, None]

Raises:

Exception – If the database query fails.

get_by_output(output: str) → OsirDbTaskModel[source]

Retrieves a single task’s details by its output. :param output: The output value to search for. :type output: str

Returns:

A dictionary containing task details, or None if no task is found.

Return type:

Union[dict, None]

Raises:

Exception – If the database query fails.

list(case_uuid: str | None = None, processing_status: str | List[str] | None = None, exclude_status: str | List[str] | None = None) → List[source]

Lists tasks based on filtering criteria such as case UUID or status.

Args:

case_uuid (str, optional): Filter tasks by a specific case UUID. processing_status (Union[str, List[str]], optional): Include only tasks with these statuses. exclude_status (Union[str, List[str]], optional): Exclude tasks with these statuses.

Returns:

list: A list of dictionaries, each representing a task.

Raises:

Exception: If the database query fails.

update(task_id: str, processing_status: ProcessingStatus, trace_data: dict | None = None, agent: str | None = None, output: dict | None = None) → dict | None[source]

Updates the status, trace data, or agent of an existing task.

Parameters:

  • task_id (str) – The UUID of the task to update.

  • processing_status (ProcessingStatus) – The new status from the ProcessingStatus enum.

  • trace_data (dict, optional) – JSON-compatible dictionary to merge into the trace column.

  • agent (str, optional) – New agent name to update.

Returns:

The updated row data (depends on the database driver’s return behavior).

Return type:

Optional[dict]

Raises:

Exception – If the update query fails.
